reasoning: YFinance shows a moderately strong multi-month downtrend historically from August 2025 (slope -1.23 per day, projecting below the 487.4 threshold) though recent days saw upward momentum (closing up at 487.35). Weekly stock movements are mostly unpredictable and behave similarly to a random walk with noise. We use the statistical downward cue to anchor slightly below 0.5 for the near term, but as the forecast horizon extends to January 2026, the variance increases and standard equity risk premium (upward drift) drives the probability back toward 0.5, obeying the max-entropy principle for longer-horizon stock forecasts.
